selected_hypotheses.summary: Multiple feature pairs exhibit perfect or near-perfect correlation (correlation=1 or ~0.9993), indicating strong redundancy and deterministic relationships among packet length, segment size, header length, flag counts, and packet counts in both forward and backward directions.
selected_hypotheses.reason: Strong correlations suggest critical redundancies impacting feature design and dimensionality reduction potential.

selected_hypotheses.summary: Backward packet length features (Max, Mean, Std) and Avg Bwd Segment Size show significant class-conditioned structural variation with high variance ratios and large differences in unique values between BENIGN and DDoS classes, indicating potential discriminative power.
selected_hypotheses.reason: Significant class-conditioned variation indicates high potential for discriminative feature utility.

selected_hypotheses.summary: Destination Port feature shows strong distributional differences between BENIGN and DDoS classes, with DDoS having a dominant ratio of 1.0 and zero entropy, while BENIGN has lower dominant ratio and higher entropy, resulting in high Jensen-Shannon divergence, indicating representation sensitivity to class-conditioned shifts.
selected_hypotheses.reason: Clear class-conditioned distribution shifts with zero entropy in attack class highlight a strong representation-sensitive signal.

deferred_hypotheses.summary: Backward bulk-related features (Bwd Avg Bulk Rate, Bwd Avg Bytes/Bulk, Bwd Avg Packets/Bulk) exhibit near-constant values with unique value counts of 1 and very low cardinality ratios, suggesting a structural uniformity or saturation effect in backward bulk traffic representation.

Backward TCP flag features (Bwd PSH Flags, Bwd URG Flags) and CWE Flag Count show minimal variability with unique values of 1, indicating a potential structural uniformity or representation insensitivity in backward flag signaling.

Forward bulk rate and packet/bulk features along with forward URG Flags show near-constant values with unique value counts of 1, suggesting a similar structural uniformity or saturation in forward traffic bulk and flag features.

Average Packet Size shows class-conditioned structural variation with moderate variance ratio and skewed unique values between BENIGN and DDoS classes, suggesting partial sensitivity to attack-related traffic changes.

The partition overview reports 729 duplicated rows and a class imbalance ratio of 1.577, indicating moderate class skew and presence of duplicate data points that may affect model training and evaluation.
Backward PSH and URG Flags show near-constant values with minimal variability, representing a weak but coherent signal of structural uniformity in backward TCP flag features.
Forward URG Flags show near-constant values with minimal variability, representing a weak but consistent signal of low variability in forward TCP flag features.
